title: OutageNotification Payload
description:
A planned or unplanned electricity outage notice. A feed is an array of
these; a push delivers one. Reuses IES conventions: Identifier
{scheme,value}, TimePeriod {start,duration}, GeoJSON geometry.

id.description: Stable notice identity; reused across UPDATE/CANCEL messages.

outageClass.description: Outage class, from the DISCOM OMS "Down Info" set (local, additive enum). Rostering = rotational load-shedding (scheduled vs emergency). Restoration is a status transition (status=RESTORED), not a class.

status.description:
Outage lifecycle state. LOCAL enum (not from a published standard);
loosely aligned with the CIM Outage lifecycle.

msgType.description: Message type; UPDATE/CANCEL refer to a prior notice via `references`.
msgType.$comment: Enum borrowed from OASIS CAP v1.2 (alert/msgType).

references.description: Prior notice ids this message updates or cancels (CAP `references`).

severity.$comment: Enum borrowed from OASIS CAP v1.2 (info/severity).

category.description: Coarse descriptor for display/filtering (local enum; not CAP `info/category`). For analytics, prefer the standardized `cause.category`.

detectedAt.description: When MDMS/SCADA first detected the outage (unplanned).

OutageCause.title: OutageCause
OutageCause.description:
Three-layer cause: a `category` + `subcategory` aligned to IEEE 1782-2022
(the standardized, cross-DISCOM dimension) plus the vendor/OMS `code`
carried verbatim (for fidelity) and free-text `text` (ODIN-style).

category.description: Standardized interruption cause category, for reliability benchmarking. Load-shedding/rostering has no standard category — map scheduled rostering to PLANNED, emergency to OTHER, and carry the nature in `outageClass`.
category.$comment: Cause categories aligned to IEEE 1782-2022 §4.4 (ten categories), used with IEEE 1366 for SAIDI/SAIFI.

subcategory.description: Standardized cause subcategory; must be valid for the chosen `category` (see $comment for the mapping). Leave unset if the field finding is inconclusive.
subcategory.$comment: Aligned to IEEE 1782-2022 §4.5; valid subcategory depends on category: EQUIPMENT={DEGRADATION,EQUIPMENT_ERROR,ENVIRONMENTAL,OTHER}; LIGHTNING={DIRECT_STRIKE,INDIRECT_STRIKE}; PLANNED={NEW_CONSTRUCTION,MAINTENANCE,CUSTOMER_REQUEST,OTHER_UTILITY_REQUEST,OTHER}; POWER_SUPPLY={GENERATION,TRANSMISSION,SUBTRANSMISSION,DISTRIBUTION,DISTRIBUTED_GENERATION_STORAGE,OTHER_UTILITY_SUPPLY}; PUBLIC={DIG_IN,FOREIGN_CONTACT,FIRE_POLICE,VEHICLE,OTHER}; VEGETATION={WITHIN_CLEARANCE_ZONE,OUTSIDE_CLEARANCE_ZONE,OTHER}; WEATHER={PRECIPITATION,ICE,WIND,EXTREME_TEMPERATURE,OTHER}; WILDLIFE={MAMMAL,BIRD,REPTILE_AMPHIBIAN,OTHER}; UNKNOWN={NO_SPECIFIC_CAUSE_FOUND}; OTHER={UTILITY_ERROR,OTHER_UTILITY_INITIATED,OTHER}.

faultType.description: Vendor asset/voltage level of the fault, e.g. 33KV, 11KV, DT, LT.

code.description: Vendor/OMS fault-reason code, carried verbatim (e.g. a DISCOM FAULT_REASON) — not standardized; map to `category`/`subcategory` for interoperability (see fault_reason_crosswalk.json). Set `codeNamespace` where the code's authority matters.

codeNamespace.description: Authority/vendor that defines `code` (e.g. the OMS vendor or DISCOM).

text.description: Free-text reason; may be localized (e.g. Hindi).

OutageAsset.title: OutageAsset
OutageAsset.description:
A network asset affected by the outage. Generic across feeder, DT,
substation, and line so new asset levels are additive.

id.description: Asset id/name; ideally a stable GIS feature id or MRID for map join.

assetLevel.description: Network level of the affected asset (local enum; aligns with CIM Equipment/UsagePoint).

consumerCategory.description: Consumer mix on the asset (local enum; DISCOM "Feeder Type").

meterRef.description: Feeder/substation smart-meter number — join key to MDMS/MeterData.

parentRef.description: Parent asset (e.g. a feeder's substation, a DT's feeder).

geo.description:
Optional inline geometry (WGS84): Point (substation/DT),
LineString (feeder route), Polygon (service area).

OutageNetworkContext.title: OutageNetworkContext
OutageNetworkContext.description:
DISCOM org hierarchy:
Discom > Zone > Circle > Division > Subdivision > Substation.
All optional for flat utilities.

OutageAffectedArea.title: OutageAffectedArea
OutageAffectedArea.description: CAP area block — free text plus structured admin areas plus GIS geometry.

geo.description: Polygon/MultiPolygon service area, or Point/circle (CAP). WGS84.

OutageImpact.title: OutageImpact
OutageImpact.description: CIM-aligned affected service points and customer counts (feeds SAIDI/SAIFI).

OutageTiming.title: OutageTiming
OutageTiming.description: Outage window and restoration timing. One canonical TimePeriod; end is derived.

actualRestoration.description: Set when status=RESTORED; feeds IEEE 1366 indices.

OutageResponse.title: OutageResponse
OutageResponse.description: Field-response and consumer-impact tracking from the OMS.

backFeedGiven.description: Partial restoration via alternate feed (OMS "Back-Feed given").

OutagePublicInfo.title: OutagePublicInfo
OutagePublicInfo.description: CAP info block — human-readable text for web publishing and push.

OutageProvenance.title: OutageProvenance
OutageProvenance.description: Link to the smart-meter alarm(s)/signal that triggered or confirmed the outage.

source.description: System that raised this notice (local enum; RTDAS = Real-Time Data Acquisition System).

amispCode.description: AMI Service Provider that supplied the detection signal (feeder-status ingest API).

detectionRef.description: Reference to the real-time detection record that raised the outage (e.g. a DISCOM RTDAS_DATA_ID). Absence or a "0" sentinel indicates a manually entered outage (source=MANUAL).

OutageSignal.title: OutageSignal
OutageSignal.description: Raw real-time feeder-status signal that triggered detection.

eventId.description: Idempotency key of the originating feeder-status event.

feederStatus.description: Normalized feeder status (local enum); raw vendor code in `rawCode`. Energized/de-energized align with CIM UsagePoint semantics.

diPort.description: Digital-input port on the substation meter that carried the signal.

rawCode.description: Vendor-native status code as received (e.g. FEEDER_STATUS=102), for traceability.

OutageAlarmRef.title: OutageAlarmRef
OutageAlarmRef.description: Pointer to a MeterData AlarmProfile entry (meter + alarmId + timestamp).

Party.title: Party
Party.description: An organisation or contact (e.g. the issuing DISCOM).

scheme.description: Identifier scheme (mixed provenance). MRID and DID are borrowed from their standards; the rest are local vendor/DISCOM master keys.
scheme.$comment: MRID borrowed from IEC 61968/61970 (CIM); DID borrowed from W3C DID Core; remaining values local.
scheme.x-standard: MRID: CIM (IEC 61968/61970); DID: W3C DID Core

TimePeriod.title: TimePeriod
TimePeriod.description: Period defined by a start time and an ISO-8601 duration.
